title: Securing a Large Monorepo
officialDescription: Your organization has migrated 30 microservices into a single GitHub monorepo shared by 5 teams. You need to enforce per-service ownership, prevent unauthorized changes to production-critical paths, detect secrets before they land in main, and gate all merges on passing security checks — without slowing down individual teams.
description: A fintech company runs payment-service, fraud-service, and compliance-service in one monorepo alongside 27 other microservices. Each service has its own team. The compliance team must approve every change to /services/compliance/. The platform team owns all GitHub Actions workflows. Secret scanning has flagged 3 historical leaks; the CISO wants zero-tolerance push protection enabled. All merges to main must pass a dependency vulnerability scan and a status check named 'security/snyk'. Deleted tags must be recoverable to support audit requirements.
architecture_notes: Use GitHub Rulesets at the organization level (not legacy Branch Protection Rules) to apply consistent enforcement across all repositories without per-repo configuration. A single 'monorepo-main' ruleset targets the main branch across all repos matching the pattern 'services-*'. CODEOWNERS uses last-match-wins: place the catch-all (*) at the top and service-specific entries below — the last matching rule wins, so /services/compliance/ @compliance-team at the bottom correctly overrides the global catch-all. Enable GHAS Secret Scanning Push Protection on the org level so pushes containing known secrets are blocked at the client before they reach the server; this is not caught by pull request checks. Add a 'dependency-review' GitHub Actions workflow triggered on pull_request that uses the actions/dependency-review-action to gate on CVSS score > 7. Register 'security/snyk' as a required status check in the Ruleset, not as a branch protection rule, so it scales org-wide. Use Rulesets 'restrict deletions' to protect tags matching 'v*' — this satisfies the audit-trail requirement without using the deprecated Tag Protection Rules API.
key_patterns: Organization-level Rulesets over per-repo Branch Protection Rules
CODEOWNERS last-match-wins: catch-all (*) at top, specific paths below
GHAS Secret Scanning Push Protection blocks at push time (not PR time)
dependency-review-action as required PR gate for CVEs
Required status check 'security/snyk' registered in Ruleset
Tag deletion protection via Rulesets 'restrict deletions' on v* pattern
Required status checks fail open by default if no CI run triggered
